enzymeStations.name: Percy (PDH Complex)
enzymeStations.description: Irreversible gatekeeper -- converts pyruvate to acetyl-CoA
enzymeStations.greeting: HALT! Name's Percy. The Dehydrated Pirate. I only sail in ONE direction.
greetingChain: Captain of the Pyruvate Dehydrogenase Complex. Irreversible. No going back.
See this hand? Count the fingers. B1... B2... B3...
Then SKIP to B5. Notice the gap? The RING finger. There IS no B4.
My ex-wife took it. But honestly? Never needed it. B4 doesn't exist.
And in its place -- this L-shaped prosthetic. Lipoic acid. The one cofactor that isn't a B vitamin.
Percy's Five: B1 Thiamine, B2 FAD, B3 NAD+, L for Lipoic, B5 CoA. Five tools. One direction.

enzymeStations.name: Sid (Citrate Synthase)
enzymeStations.description: Meticulous craftsman -- condenses Acetyl-CoA with Oxaloacetate
enzymeStations.greeting: Welcome! I'm Sid, the Citrate Synthase. I'm the FIRST enzyme of the TCA cycle. I carefully combine Acetyl-CoA with Oxaloacetate to make Citrate. It's delicate work, but someone's got to start the cycle!

enzymeStations.name: Aco (Aconitase)
enzymeStations.description: The rearranging acrobat -- isomerizes citrate to isocitrate
enzymeStations.greeting: I'm Aco, the Aconitase! I do a little rearranging act -- I flip Citrate into Isocitrate. Some people say I'm just an isomerase, but I prefer 'molecular acrobat.'

enzymeStations.name: Ike (Isocitrate DH)
enzymeStations.description: The investigator -- first CO2 release and NADH production
enzymeStations.greeting: Detective Ike here, Isocitrate Dehydrogenase. I investigate every molecule that comes my way. When I oxidize Isocitrate, I pull off a CO2 and produce the first NADH of the cycle. That's called oxidative decarboxylation -- say it three times fast!

enzymeStations.name: Alpha (Alpha-KG DH)
enzymeStations.description: Powerful complex -- second CO2 release and NADH
enzymeStations.greeting: I am Alpha, the Alpha-Ketoglutarate Dehydrogenase Complex. I am... substantial. A multi-enzyme complex, just like Percy up there. I produce the SECOND NADH and release another CO2. Together with Ike, we've now released BOTH carbons from the original Acetyl-CoA as CO2.

enzymeStations.name: Suki (Succinyl-CoA Synthetase)
enzymeStations.greeting: I'm Suki! Succinyl-CoA Synthetase. You know what makes me special? I'm the ONLY enzyme in the TCA cycle that produces a high-energy phosphate directly -- GTP! Everyone talks about oxidative phosphorylation, but I do it the old-fashioned way: substrate-level phosphorylation, baby!

enzymeStations.name: Sadie (Succinate DH)
enzymeStations.description: Dual role -- she IS Complex II of the electron transport chain!
enzymeStations.greeting: Hey there! I'm Sadie, Succinate Dehydrogenase. Fun fact about me: I'm the ONLY TCA cycle enzyme that's actually embedded in the inner mitochondrial membrane. That's because I moonlight as Complex II of the Electron Transport Chain! I produce FADH2 instead of NADH -- it feeds electrons directly into the ETC through me.

enzymeStations.name: Fuma (Fumarase)
enzymeStations.description: Bridge between worlds -- connects TCA to Urea Cycle via fumarate
enzymeStations.greeting: I'm Fuma, Fumarase! I hydrate Fumarate into Malate -- simple but essential. And here's the cool part: Fumarate also comes from the Urea Cycle! So I'm literally the BRIDGE between the Urea Cycle and the TCA Cycle. Two worlds, connected through me.

malateDH.name: Mal (Malate DH)
malateDH.description: The closer -- produces the third NADH and regenerates Oxaloacetate
malateDH.greeting: I'm Mal, Malate Dehydrogenase. I'm the last enzyme of the cycle. I oxidize Malate to regenerate Oxaloacetate -- which goes right back to Sid to start the cycle again! I also produce the THIRD and final NADH. The cycle is complete!
